title: scurrula fasciculata
intro: Scurrula fasciculata, also known as the Indian mistletoe or mistletoe of the genus Scurrula, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, usually a species of Ficus. The medicinal properties of Scurrula fasciculata lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and fever, as well as its antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Scurrula fasciculata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Ayurvedic and Unani systems.
